# Distill ▲ Graph

> A knowledge graph of 36 reusable engineering patterns, distilled from 7 open-source repositories into 20 domains. This file is the machine-readable representation of the graph for AI agents and crawlers. The visual app at https://distill-graph.vercel.app renders the same data as an interactive star map.

## At a glance
- Patterns: 36
- Domains: 20
- Repositories: 7
- Connections: 84 (avg 4.7 per pattern)
- Source of truth: https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it
- Raw data (JSON): https://distill-graph.vercel.app/graph.json
- Visual map: https://distill-graph.vercel.app  ·  Stats/analysis: https://distill-graph.vercel.app/stats

## How to read a pattern
Each pattern has a label, a domain, a source repository, a one-line summary, a plain-language study doc, and a build spec (a self-contained reimplementation guide). Study and build links point into the distill-it repository.

## The backbone (most connected patterns, study these first)
1. Converter Pipeline (11 connections) — document-conversion, from markitdown
2. Multi-Source Research Engine (10 connections) — research-automation, from last30days-skill
3. Schema-Driven Extraction (10 connections) — structured-extraction, from llm-scraper
4. AI Rules Engine (10 connections) — ai-automation, from inbox-zero
5. Ordered Backend Routing (10 connections) — agent-architecture, from agent-reach
6. Page Format Pipeline (7 connections) — content-preprocessing, from llm-scraper

## Foundations (most depended-upon, highest-leverage to reuse)
- Email Provider Abstraction (4 patterns depend on it) — from inbox-zero
- Page Format Pipeline (3 patterns depend on it) — from llm-scraper
- Provider-Agnostic LLM Layer (3 patterns depend on it) — from llm-scraper
- Cookie Credential Extraction (3 patterns depend on it) — from agent-reach
- HTML Web Conversion (2 patterns depend on it) — from markitdown
- Multi-Source Research Engine (2 patterns depend on it) — from last30days-skill

## Patterns by domain

### Document Conversion (4)
Converting structured documents (PDFs, Office files, archives, plaintext) into clean Markdown for downstream text analysis or LLM consumption.

- **Converter Pipeline** — from markitdown. Priority-sorted pluggable converter registry. Source-type dispatch → layered file detection → sorted converter chain. First accepts+succeeds wins. Output normalized to clean Markdown.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/converter-pipeline--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/converter-pipeline--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **Office Doc Conversion** — from markitdown. DOCX/PPTX/XLSX → Markdown via python-docx, python-pptx, pandas. Tables go HTML→Markdown via HtmlConverter.convert_string(). PPTX handles charts, speaker notes, and optional LLM image captions.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/office-doc-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/office-doc-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **PDF Conversion** — from markitdown. Dual-library PDF extraction: pdfplumber primary (table/form column detection, page.close() memory management) → pdfminer fallback. Markdown tables from column-clustered word positions.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/pdf-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/pdf-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **ZIP Archive Traversal** — from markitdown. Recursive ZIP extraction: iterate entries → BytesIO streams → call parent pipeline on each file. Silent skip on conversion failure. Nested ZIPs handled automatically.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/study/zip-archive-traversal--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/document-conversion/build/zip-archive-traversal--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

### Research Automation (3)
parallel multi-source retrieval, entity resolution before search, engagement-based ranking, and graceful degradation when sources fail.

- **Multi-Source Research Engine** — from last30days-skill. ThreadPoolExecutor fan-out to 6–20 sources. Depth tiers (quick/default/deep) cap source count. 2-phase retrieval with 5xx retry. Unified Report schema. Five emit formats (compact/json/context/md/html).
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/research-automation/study/multi-source-research-engine--from-last30days-skill.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/research-automation/build/multi-source-research-engine--from-last30days-skill.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill)

- **Entity Resolution** — from last30days-skill. 4 parallel searches resolve topic → {subreddits, handles, repos, context}. X handles get 3× URL weight. GitHub -action canonicalization. News context capped at 300 chars for LLM injection.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/research-automation/study/entity-resolution--from-last30days-skill.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/research-automation/build/entity-resolution--from-last30days-skill.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill)

- **Engagement Signal Ranking** — from last30days-skill. Two-stage: RRF (k=60, topic-sensitive source_weights) then LLM rerank of top-N. Quality nudge detects degraded sources via domain diversity + snippet density. All inputs from pipeline Report.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/research-automation/study/engagement-signal-ranking--from-last30days-skill.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/research-automation/build/engagement-signal-ranking--from-last30days-skill.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill)

### Tts (3)
on-device / local text-to-speech — turning text into a speech waveform without a cloud API.

- **ONNX TTS Pipeline** — from supertonic. On-device TTS as four chained ONNX models: duration_predictor -> text_encoder -> vector_estimator (flow-matching, looped) -> vocoder. Unicode-code-point tokenizer covers 31 languages; voice is a small style JSON; outputs 44.1kHz WAV, no GPU.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/tts/study/onnx-tts-pipeline--from-supertonic.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/tts/build/onnx-tts-pipeline--from-supertonic.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/supertone-inc/supertonic)

- **Flow-Matching Sampler** — from supertonic. Generative core: iteratively denoise a gaussian latent into a speech latent via vector_estimator.onnx, called total_steps times (5-12, default 8). current_step/total_step are tensor inputs, giving a runtime quality/latency dial without retraining. Length-Aware RoPE keeps text<->speech aligned.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/tts/study/flow-matching-sampler--from-supertonic.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/tts/build/flow-matching-sampler--from-supertonic.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/supertone-inc/supertonic)

- **Expression Tags** — from supertonic. Inline prosody control via ~10 closed-vocabulary text tags (<laugh>, <breath>, <sigh>). Recognized in the text front-end and mapped to reserved token ids, so they flow through every stage as ordinary tokens and the trained models render them as acoustic events. No DSP, no reference audio.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/tts/study/expression-tags--from-supertonic.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/tts/build/expression-tags--from-supertonic.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/supertone-inc/supertonic)

### Agent Architecture (2)
separating prose contracts from executable engines, enforcing output consistency across harnesses, and designing for cross-platform compatibility.

- **Ordered Backend Routing** — from agent-reach. Each capability is a Channel with an ORDERED backends list (preferred→fallbacks). Two-phase check: collect all candidates, first `ok` wins, then first `warn` — so an installed-but-logged-out backend can't mask a working fallback. active_backend records the live one. User pin via <name>_backend (unknown value ignored). Switching backend = reorder a list, not code.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-architecture/study/ordered-backend-routing--from-agent-reach.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-architecture/build/ordered-backend-routing--from-agent-reach.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Panniantong/Agent-Reach)

- **Agent Output Contract** — from last30days-skill. 5-law output contract enforced via SKILL.md prompt text: no Sources block, no invented headers, space-dash-space separators, no ## inside body sections, verbatim footer passthrough. Zero runtime validation.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-architecture/study/agent-output-contract--from-last30days-skill.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-architecture/build/agent-output-contract--from-last30days-skill.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill)

### Ai Automation (2)
How software turns natural-language user intent into structured, automated actions over a stream of incoming items — with an LLM in the loop for the judgment calls and deterministic code for everything that can be decided cheaply.

- **AI Rules Engine** — from inbox-zero. Plain-English inbox automation. Cost funnel: static/pattern matching first (address-anchored, AND/OR), LLM rule-selection only for the ambiguous remainder (Zod schema + 'no match' flag), second LLM call fills {{template}} action args, deterministic executor runs actions + writes ExecutedRule/ExecutedAction audit trail. Dry-run mode skips all writes.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/study/ai-rules-engine--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/build/ai-rules-engine--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

- **AI Reply Drafting** — from inbox-zero. Tone-matched reply drafts + confidence (LOW/MED/HIGH). Assembles thread (per-msg ~3000 char cap), profile, knowledge, calendar slots, past drafts. Writing-style priority: explicit > learned > default. Learned style is a distilled PII-free summary (<=1500 chars) of the user's edits. Output normalizer + repetition guard.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/study/ai-reply-drafting--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-automation/build/ai-reply-drafting--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

### Ai Integration (2)
Wiring external AI services (cloud OCR, LLM vision, speech recognition, provider-agnostic LLM SDKs) into a product — as enhancement layers in a pipeline, or as the swappable engine a whole tool is built on.

- **Provider-Agnostic LLM Layer** — from llm-scraper. Multi-provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Groq/Ollama) with zero per-provider code by depending on the Vercel AI SDK LanguageModel abstraction. Constructor takes one model handle; provider chosen by caller. Lesson: abstract one level above the vendor.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-integration/study/provider-agnostic-llm--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-integration/build/provider-agnostic-llm--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

- **Azure Doc Intelligence** — from markitdown. Azure prebuilt-layout model for OCR-grade extraction of PDFs, images, Office files. Submits bytes, gets native Markdown back, strips HTML comments. OCR features (high-res, formulas, fonts) enabled only for PDF/image types.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-integration/study/azure-doc-intelligence--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/ai-integration/build/azure-doc-intelligence--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

### Content Preprocessing (2)
Reducing raw, messy source material into a clean, compact representation an LLM can read cheaply and accurately — before any extraction or reasoning happens.

- **Page Format Pipeline** — from llm-scraper. preprocess(page,options) → {url,content,format}. Six formats: raw_html, markdown (Turndown), text (Mozilla Readability via CDN in-page), html (cleanup scrub, default), image (base64 screenshot), custom. Cost/fidelity dial.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/study/page-format-pipeline--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/build/page-format-pipeline--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

- **HTML Cleanup** — from llm-scraper. In-browser DOM scrub via page.evaluate. Removes ~30 tag types (script/style/media/form/nav/head) and prefix-strips noise attributes (style/src/on*/aria-/data-). Mutates live DOM. href kept. Token-diet for the default html format.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/study/html-cleanup--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-preprocessing/build/html-cleanup--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

### Credential Management (2)
Patterns for managing multi-tier authentication in tools that aggregate from heterogeneous sources — keyless public APIs, browser-cookie sessions, and paid API keys — with graceful degradation when credentials are absent.

- **Cookie Credential Extraction** — from agent-reach. Multi-browser cookie harvest (rookiepy→browser_cookie3) driven by declarative per-platform specs (named cookies vs whole Cookie-header). Validate-before-save: require a logged-in marker (auth_token+ct0, xq_a_token) — presence≠valid. Atomic 0o600 write (O_CREAT+mode, no write-then-chmod race). shlex-quoted best-effort mirrors into upstream tools' credential files.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/credential-management/study/cookie-credential-extraction--from-agent-reach.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/credential-management/build/cookie-credential-extraction--from-agent-reach.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Panniantong/Agent-Reach)

- **Multi-Tier Credentials** — from last30days-skill. Tier 1 keyless (Reddit/HN/Polymarket/GitHub), Tier 2 browser cookies (X via Bird/Chrome/Safari/xurl, YouTube via yt-dlp/SSH), Tier 3 API keys (.env dual hierarchy, perms 600). Preflight startup checks.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/credential-management/study/multi-tier-credentials--from-last30days-skill.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/credential-management/build/multi-tier-credentials--from-last30days-skill.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill)

### Inbox Cleanup (2)
unsubscribing from unwanted senders, mass-archiving low-value mail, and tracking per-sender status so the system stops re-bothering the user.

- **Bulk Archiver** — from inbox-zero. Risk-tiered archive triage. Pure function scoring senders by category-name substring: HIGH (marketing/promotion/newsletter/sale), MEDIUM (notification/alert/receipt/update), LOW (other). Returns {confidence, reason} candidates. Only HIGH is one-click — tiering is the safety mechanism. Scoring decoupled from execution.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/study/bulk-archiver--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/build/bulk-archiver--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

- **Bulk Unsubscriber** — from inbox-zero. Real server-side one-click unsubscribe. Parses RFC 2369 List-Unsubscribe (extracts https URL), RFC 8058 one-click POST (body 'List-Unsubscribe=One-Click') with GET fallback, SSRF-guarded fetch, <=5 redirects with 301/302/303->GET downgrade. Persists per-sender Newsletter status (APPROVED/UNSUBSCRIBED/AUTO_ARCHIVED).
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/study/bulk-unsubscriber--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/inbox-cleanup/build/bulk-unsubscriber--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

### Media Processing (2)
Extracting text, metadata, and AI-generated descriptions from binary media files (images, audio) for use in text pipelines.

- **Image + LLM Captioning** — from markitdown. JPEG/PNG converter with two optional layers: exiftool subprocess for EXIF metadata, OpenAI-compatible vision API for image description. Both independently optional. Output: key-values + ## Description.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/media-processing/study/image-llm-captioning--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/media-processing/build/image-llm-captioning--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **Audio Transcription** — from markitdown. WAV/MP3/M4A/MP4 → Markdown via speech_recognition (Google Web Speech API) + exiftool metadata. Duration excluded (wrong from memory stream). Temp file needed for non-WAV formats.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/media-processing/study/audio-transcription--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/media-processing/build/audio-transcription--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

### Structured Extraction (2)
Turning unstructured content (a webpage, a document, a blob of text) into typed, schema-validated data using an LLM as the extraction engine.

- **Schema-Driven Extraction** — from llm-scraper. Playwright page + Zod/JSON schema → typed object. Preprocess page, then AI SDK generateText with schema as `output` constraint, read result.output. Schema is both TS type and runtime constraint. One method: run(). No retry loop.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/structured-extraction/study/schema-driven-extraction--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/structured-extraction/build/schema-driven-extraction--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

- **Streaming Partial Objects** — from llm-scraper. stream() variant: AI SDK streamText with schema output → partialOutputStream async iterable. Each emission is a schema-conformant partial; last is complete. Mirror of run() but returns {stream,url}. For responsive UIs.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/structured-extraction/study/streaming-partial-objects--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/structured-extraction/build/streaming-partial-objects--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

### Web Extraction (2)
Turning web resources — HTML pages, YouTube videos, RSS feeds, Wikipedia articles — into clean Markdown by combining HTTP fetching, DOM parsing, and platform-specific API calls.

- **HTML Web Conversion** — from markitdown. BeautifulSoup + custom markdownify subclass. Strips scripts/styles, extracts body, RecursionError fallback to plaintext. convert_string() helper used by DOCX/PPTX/XLSX for table rendering. Wikipedia/RSS/Bing specializations.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/web-extraction/study/html-web-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/web-extraction/build/html-web-conversion--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

- **YouTube Extraction** — from markitdown. YouTube watch URL → Markdown. Meta tag scrape for views/keywords/duration. Recursive ytInitialData JSON search for description. youtube_transcript_api with 3-retry logic for transcript.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/web-extraction/study/youtube-extraction--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/web-extraction/build/youtube-extraction--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

### Adaptive Parsing (1)
Features that make data extraction survive change — selectors, parsers, or extractors that don't break the moment a source (a website, a document format, an API response shape) is restructured.

- **Adaptive Element Relocation** — from scrapling. Fingerprints an element (tag/text/attrs/siblings/path/parent) on first scrape, then re-finds it by fuzzy similarity scoring when the selector later breaks. Default 40% threshold. SQLite-backed, pluggable storage.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/adaptive-parsing/study/adaptive-element-relocation--from-scrapling.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/adaptive-parsing/build/adaptive-element-relocation--from-scrapling.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/D4Vinci/Scrapling)

### Agent Distribution (1)
Patterns for shipping, installing, and maintaining software through an AI agent — where a versioned natural-language runbook is the installer, bounded by explicit safety guardrails, and the same mechanism handles updates and ongoing health.

- **Agent-Driven Install** — from agent-reach. The installer is a markdown runbook an LLM agent executes: thin 'For Humans' one-line trigger over an imperative 'For AI Agents' program. Hard boundaries (no sudo, confined to ~/.agent-reach/, never pollute the workspace), env-branched steps (PEP 668 / Homebrew / Windows Store-alias), explicit user checkpoints, 'selector/router, never a wrapper'. install.md / update.md / daily watch = one lifecycle mechanism.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-distribution/study/agent-driven-install--from-agent-reach.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/agent-distribution/build/agent-driven-install--from-agent-reach.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Panniantong/Agent-Reach)

### Code Generation (1)
Using an LLM to write runnable code as the deliverable, rather than to produce data directly. The model's output is a program — typically to be executed afterward — that does the actual work.

- **Scraper Code Generation** — from llm-scraper. generate() asks LLM to write a self-contained IIFE JS scraper for page+schema instead of extracting. Plain generateText (schema as context, not constraint). stripMarkdownBackticks cleans fences. Amortizes AI cost across runs.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/code-generation/study/scraper-code-generation--from-llm-scraper.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/code-generation/build/scraper-code-generation--from-llm-scraper.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mishushakov/llm-scraper)

### Content Synthesis (1)
Turning raw multi-source retrieval results into coherent, deduplicated, ranked summaries — clustering duplicate stories, selecting diverse representatives, and generating shareable output formats.

- **Cross-Source Clustering** — from last30days-skill. Two-pass clustering: Pass 1 greedy text similarity (0.42/0.48 thresholds), Pass 2 entity overlap on small clusters. MMR picks up to 3 reps per cluster. Polymarket isolation, same-source enforcement.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/study/cross-source-clustering--from-last30days-skill.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/content-synthesis/build/cross-source-clustering--from-last30days-skill.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill)

### Diagnostics (1)
Patterns for answering "is this actually working right now?" — health probing of external dependencies, survivable aggregation into a human/agent-readable report, and turning failures into actionable, specific fixes.

- **Channel Health Diagnostics** — from agent-reach. probe_command EXECUTES a side-effect-free command to classify missing/broken(stale venv shebang, exit 126/127)/timeout/ok — which() existence is not health. doctor aggregates per-channel (exceptions degrade to error, stale active_backend cleared), groups by tier, annotates the active backend only when >1 exists, and audits config.yaml permissions (chmod 600). Retries only transient failures.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/diagnostics/study/channel-health-diagnostics--from-agent-reach.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/diagnostics/build/channel-health-diagnostics--from-agent-reach.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/Panniantong/Agent-Reach)

### Email Platform (1)
a single, provider-agnostic interface that wraps Gmail and Outlook/Microsoft Graph behind one set of methods, plus the OAuth token plumbing that keeps those clients alive.

- **Email Provider Abstraction** — from inbox-zero. Adapter+factory seam over Gmail + Outlook/Graph. One EmailProvider interface, GmailProvider/OutlookProvider adapters normalize to a shared ParsedMessage. createEmailProvider validates provider, fails fast on rate-limit, gets OAuth client (token refresh hidden in client getters). Label vs folder absorbed by adapters.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/email-platform/study/email-provider-abstraction--from-inbox-zero.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/email-platform/build/email-provider-abstraction--from-inbox-zero.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/elie222/inbox-zero)

### File Detection (1)
Determining a file's true type from content, extension, MIME type hints, and ML-based identification — without trusting the filename alone.

- **Magika File Detection** — from markitdown. Layered type detection: extension+MIME hints → mimetypes bidirectional enrichment → Magika ML content scan → charset_normalizer for text files. Returns ranked StreamInfo list.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/file-detection/study/magika-file-detection--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/file-detection/build/magika-file-detection--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

### Plugin Architecture (1)
Patterns for making a library extensible by third parties via Python entry points, without modifying the core package.

- **Plugin System** — from markitdown. importlib.metadata entry_points discovery under group 'markitdown.plugin'. Lazy load with fault isolation. register_converters(host, **kwargs) callback pattern. __plugin_interface_version__ for forward compat.
  [study](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/plugin-architecture/study/plugin-system--from-markitdown.md) · [build spec](https://github.com/derrick-ships/distill-it/blob/main/features/plugin-architecture/build/plugin-system--from-markitdown.md) · [origin repo](https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown)

## Repositories (by contribution)
- markitdown: 11 patterns
- last30days-skill: 6 patterns
- llm-scraper: 6 patterns
- inbox-zero: 5 patterns
- agent-reach: 4 patterns
- supertonic: 3 patterns
- scrapling: 1 pattern

## Relationship types
- depends-on (34): one pattern needs another to work
- same-repo (26): shipped in the same project
- same-domain (11): solves a related problem
- similar-pattern (9): an independent take on the same idea
- alternative-to (4): a competing approach

## Convergent solutions (same problem, different repos, worth comparing)
- PDF Conversion vs Azure Doc Intelligence (markitdown / markitdown)
- Image + LLM Captioning vs Azure Doc Intelligence (markitdown / markitdown)
- Scraper Code Generation vs Schema-Driven Extraction (llm-scraper / llm-scraper)
- Schema-Driven Extraction ~ Adaptive Element Relocation (llm-scraper / scrapling)
- HTML Cleanup ~ HTML Web Conversion (llm-scraper / markitdown)
- Provider-Agnostic LLM Layer vs Azure Doc Intelligence (llm-scraper / markitdown)
- Email Provider Abstraction ~ Provider-Agnostic LLM Layer (inbox-zero / llm-scraper)
- AI Rules Engine ~ Schema-Driven Extraction (inbox-zero / llm-scraper)
- AI Rules Engine ~ Engagement Signal Ranking (inbox-zero / last30days-skill)
- Ordered Backend Routing ~ Plugin System (agent-reach / markitdown)
- Ordered Backend Routing ~ Email Provider Abstraction (agent-reach / inbox-zero)
- Channel Health Diagnostics ~ Multi-Tier Credentials (agent-reach / last30days-skill)
- Agent-Driven Install ~ Agent Output Contract (agent-reach / last30days-skill)
